articles.title: AI Turns Polls And Prediction Markets Into A New Battleground
articles.summary: AI is now being weaponized to manipulate both traditional polling and prediction markets, creating a new front in the information warfare that surrounds major elections and events. Bettors face a landscape where distinguishing genuine sentiment from algorithmic noise becomes critical to avoiding market manipulation designed to separate them from their money.

articles.title: An Evening at Polymarket’s Totally Broken Pop-up Bar
articles.summary: Polymarket threw a pop-up bar event that apparently went sideways, with broken systems or operations that left attendees frustrated. The incident highlights how the platform's real-world marketing efforts might be as glitchy as their occasional technical issues, potentially undermining user confidence just as they're trying to mainstream prediction markets.

articles.title: Strait of Hormuz tanker traffic won't return to normal for months, Kalshi bettors predict
articles.summary: Kalshi traders are betting that tanker traffic through the critical Strait of Hormuz will remain disrupted for months, not weeks. The market signal suggests bettors expect prolonged geopolitical tensions or infrastructure damage that could keep oil prices elevated and shipping routes unstable.

articles.title: More Evidence Emerges of Iran War Insider Trading
articles.summary: New reports suggest traders with advance knowledge of Iran's military actions have been profiting from strategic bets on defense stocks, oil futures, and geopolitical markets. If proven, this pattern could signal that prediction markets are being systematically gamed by those with access to classified intelligence, making crowd wisdom less reliable when it matters most.

articles.title: U.S. regulators eye rules for prediction markets
articles.summary: U.S. financial regulators are considering new rules to govern how prediction markets operate, potentially affecting platforms like Kalshi, PredictIt, and the growing crypto betting scene. Any regulatory framework could either legitimize prediction markets for mainstream adoption or strangle them with compliance costs and trading restrictions.

articles.title: Exclusive: Trader made nearly $1 million on Polymarket with remarkably accurate Iran bets
articles.summary: A trader scored nearly $1 million on Polymarket by nailing a series of Iran-related bets with uncanny precision. This shows how geopolitical expertise can translate into massive prediction market profits, likely attracting more serious money and talent to these platforms.

articles.title: Lawmakers Introduce Bipartisan Bill Banning Sports Bets on Prediction Markets
articles.summary: Bipartisan lawmakers just introduced legislation to ban sports betting on prediction markets, targeting platforms like Kalshi and PredictIt that offer wagers on game outcomes. The bill threatens to carve out a massive revenue stream from prediction markets just as they're gaining mainstream traction, potentially forcing platforms to choose between sports content and regulatory compliance.

articles.title: Bets on US-Iran ceasefire show signs of insider knowledge, say experts
articles.summary: Prediction markets betting on a US-Iran ceasefire are showing suspicious trading patterns that experts believe indicate traders have access to non-public diplomatic information. This raises fresh concerns about information asymmetry in geopolitical markets, where connected insiders can exploit advance knowledge of sensitive negotiations to generate outsized returns.

articles.title: Polymarket’s Coming-Out Party in Washington Was a Disaster
articles.summary: Polymarket's first major Washington DC event to court regulators and legitimize prediction markets went sideways, likely featuring awkward exchanges between crypto-betting executives and skeptical policymakers. The fumbled debut signals that regulatory acceptance remains elusive, which could keep institutional money on the sidelines and force serious bettors to stick with overseas platforms.

articles.title: Opinion | We Shouldn’t Want a World Run by Prediction Markets
articles.summary: Someone's arguing that prediction markets shouldn't become the dominant force in decision-making across society. This pushback matters because it signals growing concern about market-based governance as prediction platforms gain mainstream credibility and political influence.
